mnemonics.title: Use Case Components
mnemonics.content: Remember the 3 A's: Actor, Action (Use Case), and Application Boundary (System). Just like a play has actors, a script (use case), and a stage (boundary).

mnemonics.title: Use Case Flow Types
mnemonics.content: B.A.S.I.C = Basic flow (Happy path), Alternative flows, System interactions, Inclusion of common behavior, Conditions for extensions. Another way: Think of Basic as the 'main road' and Alternatives as 'detours'.

mnemonics.title: Include vs Extend
mnemonics.content: INCLUDE = INcorporate (reuse common behavior like borrowing a common utility). EXTEND = EXTRA behavior added when needed (like optional toppings on a pizza).

mnemonics.title: Pre vs Post Conditions
mnemonics.content: PRE = Before (PRoactive conditions that must be met BEFORE starting). POST = After (the PROud result that remains AFTER successful completion).

mnemonics.title: Actor Types
mnemonics.content: PRIMARY = Proactive (starts the action, gets the profit). SECONDARY = Supportive (helps the system function).

acronyms.usage: The standard modeling language used for visualizing use case diagrams and other software designs.
The interface through which actors (users) often interact with the system.
Common basic operations that many use cases involve when managing data entities.
Secondary actors (external systems) often interact with the system through APIs.

visualTips.visualization: Draw actors on the left and right outside the system boundary. Place the system as a rectangle in the center. Connect actors to use cases with solid lines. Draw include/extend relationships as dashed arrows inside the boundary.

<<include>> = Dashed arrow with umbrella-like stereotype pointing to the included (reused) behavior. <<extend>> = Dashed arrow with <<extend>> pointing to the base use case. Generalization = Solid triangle arrowhead pointing to parent.

Think of it like a recipe: Title (dish name), Actors (who's cooking), Preconditions (ingredients needed), Basic Flow (cooking steps), Alternative Flows (what to do if something goes wrong), Postconditions (the final dish).

analogies.analogy: A use case is like a scene in a movie - it describes what happens when a character (actor) interacts with the setting (system) to achieve a goal.
Actors in use cases are like characters in a play - they have roles and interact with the stage (system) but are not part of the stage itself.
Think of include as a shared utility function - like a photocopy machine used by different departments to avoid each department buying their own.
Extend is like ordering extras at a restaurant - the base meal (basic flow) always exists, but you can add fries or drinks (extensions) under certain conditions.

analogies.anal analogy: The system boundary is like the stage in theater - everything inside is part of the performance (system), while the audience (actors) watches from outside.
